steps.title: Locate the marked angle at line n
steps.content: Where transversal k crosses line n, an angle of 135° is explicitly marked in the lower-left region (below line n and to the left of k). At any line-transversal intersection, the four angles consist of two pairs: vertical angles are equal, and adjacent angles are supplementary. Since one angle is 135°, the four angles measure 135°, 45°, 135°, and 45°.

steps.title: Identify angle s
steps.content: The label s is positioned directly at the 135° angle at the intersection of k and n. Since s labels that angle, s = 135°. By corresponding angles (when a transversal crosses parallel lines, angles in the same relative position are equal), the angle in the corresponding position at line m is also 135°. That matches choice C — C is correct.
